{"parentUuid":"4e995259-1d4f-489f-9cc5-48a66d384841","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"e8ea9607-3edf-47ab-841d-b275b29399eb","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"tool_result","tool_use_id":"toolu_011Sw2FHmjaayMamaadexVfv","content":"Launching skill: code-review"}]},"uuid":"2ccbe7c2-70de-4c02-842c-2100f0dc6cb5","timestamp":"2026-06-06T18:57:15.664Z","toolUseResult":{"success":true,"commandName":"code-review"},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"4e995259-1d4f-489f-9cc5-48a66d384841","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור","sessionId":"368947d7-a64b-4fc9-a2f6-fccfabe83369","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"master"}
{"parentUuid":"2ccbe7c2-70de-4c02-842c-2100f0dc6cb5","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"e8ea9607-3edf-47ab-841d-b275b29399eb","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"`high effort → 3+4 angles × 6 candidates → 1-vote verify (recall-biased) → ≤10 findings`\n\nYou are reviewing for **recall** at high effort: catch every real bug a careful\nreviewer would catch in one sitting. At this level, catching real bugs matters\nmore than avoiding false positives. Err on the side of surfacing.\n\n## Phase 0 — Gather the diff\n\nRun `git diff @{upstream}...HEAD` (or `git diff main...HEAD` / `git diff HEAD~1`\nif there's no upstream) to get the unified diff under review. If there are\nuncommitted changes, or the range diff is empty, also run `git diff HEAD` and\ninclude the working-tree changes in scope — the review often runs before the\ncommit. If a PR number, branch name, or file path was passed as an argument,\nreview that target instead. Treat this diff as the review scope.\n\n## Phase 1 — Find candidates (3 correctness angles + 3 cleanup angles + 1 altitude angle, up to 6 each)\n\nRun **7 independent finder angles** via the Agent tool. Each\nsurfaces **up to 6 candidate findings** with `file`, `line`, a one-line\n`summary`, and a concrete `failure_scenario`.\n\n### Angle A — line-by-line diff scan\n\nRead every hunk in the diff, line by line. Then Read the enclosing function for\neach hunk — bugs in unchanged lines of a touched function are in scope (the PR\nre-exposes or fails to fix them). For every line ask: what input, state, timing,\nor platform makes this line wrong? Look for inverted/wrong conditions,\noff-by-one, null/undefined deref, missing `await`, falsy-zero checks,\nwrong-variable copy-paste, error swallowed in catch, unescaped regex metachars.\n\n### Angle B — removed-behavior auditor\n\nFor every line the diff DELETES or replaces, name the invariant or behavior it\nenforced, then search the new code for where that invariant is re-established.\nIf you can't find it, that's a candidate: a removed guard, a dropped error\npath, a narrowed validation, a deleted test that was covering a real case.\n\n### Angle C — cross-file tracer\n\nFor each function the diff changes, find its callers (Grep for the symbol) and\ncheck whether the change breaks any call site: a new precondition, a changed\nreturn shape, a new exception, a timing/ordering dependency. Also check callees:\ndoes a parallel change in the same PR make a call unsafe?\n\n### Reuse\n\nThe angles above hunt for bugs; this one and the next two hunt for cleanup in\nthe changed code. Flag new code that re-implements something the codebase\nalready has — Grep shared/utility modules and files adjacent to the change,\nand name the existing helper to call instead.\n\n### Simplification\n\nFlag unnecessary complexity the diff adds: redundant or derivable state,\ncopy-paste with slight variation, deep nesting, dead code left behind. Name\nthe simpler form that does the same job.\n\n### Efficiency\n\nFlag wasted work the diff introduces: redundant computation or repeated I/O,\nindependent operations run sequentially, blocking work added to startup or\nhot paths. Name the cheaper alternative.\n\n### Altitude\n\nCheck that each change is implemented at the right depth, not as a fragile\nbandaid. Special cases layered on shared infrastructure are a sign the fix\nisn't deep enough — prefer generalizing the underlying mechanism over adding\nspecial cases.\n\nCleanup and altitude candidates use the same `file`/`line`/`summary` shape; in\n`failure_scenario`, state the concrete cost (what is duplicated, wasted, or\nharder to maintain) instead of a crash. Correctness bugs always outrank\ncleanup and altitude findings when the output cap forces a cut.\n\nPass every candidate with a nameable failure scenario through — finders that\nsilently drop half-believed candidates bypass the verify step and are the\ndominant cause of misses.\n\n## Phase 2 — Verify (1-vote, recall-biased)\n\nDedup near-duplicates (same defect, same location, same reason → keep one). For\neach remaining candidate, run **one verifier** via the Agent tool:\ngive it the diff, the relevant file(s), and the candidate; it returns exactly\none of **CONFIRMED / PLAUSIBLE / REFUTED**.\n\n**PLAUSIBLE by default** — do not refute a candidate for being \"speculative\" or\n\"depends on runtime state\" when the state is realistic: concurrency races,\nnil/undefined on a rare-but-reachable path (error handler, cold cache, missing\noptional field), falsy-zero treated as missing, off-by-one on a boundary the\ncode does not exclude, retry storms / partial failures, regex/allowlist that\nlost an anchor. These are PLAUSIBLE.\n\n**REFUTED** only when constructible from the code: factually wrong (quote the\nactual line); provably impossible (type/constant/invariant — show it); already\nhandled in this diff (cite the guard); or pure style with no observable effect.\n\nKeep **CONFIRMED and PLAUSIBLE**. Drop REFUTED.\n\n## Output\n\nReturn findings as a JSON array of at most 10 objects:\n\n```json\n[\n  {\n    \"file\": \"path/to/file.ext\",\n    \"line\": 123,\n    \"summary\": \"one-sentence statement of the bug\",\n    \"failure_scenario\": \"concrete inputs/state → wrong output/crash\"\n  }\n]\n```\n\nRanked most-severe first. If more than 10 survive, keep the 10 most\nsevere. If nothing survives verification, return `[]`.\n"}]},"isMeta":true,"uuid":"91c820cc-5ed2-4dec-8e6a-4efc16a63738","timestamp":"2026-06-06T18:57:15.657Z","sourceToolUseID":"toolu_011Sw2FHmjaayMamaadexVfv","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור","sessionId":"368947d7-a64b-4fc9-a2f6-fccfabe83369","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"master"}

Use this skill when asked to perform real-time search across web, academic, medical, transportation, financial sources, content extraction from URLs, AI-powered answers with citations, or comprehensive deep research reports.\n+license: MIT\n+compatibility: Requires network access and valid Valyu API key\n+metadata:\n+  author: valyu\n+  version: \"1.0\"\n+---\n+\n+# Valyu Best Practices\n+\n+This skill provides instructions for using the Valyu API to perform search, content extraction, AI-powered answers, and deep research tasks.\n+\n+## Quick Reference: Choosing the Right API\n+\n+Use this decision tree to select the appropriate Valyu API:\n+\n+```\n+What do you need?\n+\n+├─ Find information across multiple sources\n+│  └─ Use Search API\n+│\n+├─ Extract content from specific URLs\n+│  └─ Use Contents API\n+│\n+├─ Get an AI-synthesized answer with citations\n+│  └─ Use Answer API\n+│\n+├─ Generate a comprehensive research report\n+│  └─ Use DeepResearch API\n+│\n+└─ Discover available data sources\n+   └─ Use Datasources API\n+```\n+\n+---\n+\n+## ⚠️ MANDATORY: Use Official Valyu SDK Libraries\n+\n+**CRITICAL: When writing code that uses the Valyu API, you MUST use the official SDK libraries. NEVER make raw HTTP/fetch calls to the Valyu API endpoints.**\n+\n+### JavaScript/TypeScript: `valyu-js`\n+\n+```bash\n+npm install valyu-js\n+# or\n+pnpm add valyu-js\n+```\n+\n+```typescript\n+import { Valyu } from 'valyu-js';\n+\n+const valyu = new Valyu(process.env.VALYU_API_KEY);\n+\n+// Now use valyu.search(), valyu.contents(), valyu.answer(), valyu.deepResearch\n+```\n+\n+### Python: `valyu`\n+\n+```bash\n+pip install valyu\n+# or\n+uv add valyu\n+```\n+\n+```python\n+from valyu import Valyu\n+\n+valyu = Valyu(api_key=os.environ.get(\"VALYU_API_KEY\"))\n+\n+# Now use valyu.search(), valyu.contents(), valyu.answer(), valyu.deep_research\n+```\n+\n+### Why SDK Over Raw API Calls?\n+\n+1. **Type safety** - Full TypeScript/Python type hints for all parameters and responses\n+2. **Automatic retries** - Built-in retry logic for transient failures\n+3. **Streaming support** - Proper async iterator support for streaming responses\n+4. **Error handling** - Structured error types with helpful messages\n+5. **Future compatibility** - SDK updates handle API changes automatically\n+\n+### ❌ NEVER Do This\n+\n+```typescript\n+// DON'T make raw fetch calls\n+const response = await fetch('https://api.valyu.ai/v1/search', {\n+  method: 'POST',\n+  headers: {\n+    'x-api-key': apiKey,\n+    'Content-Type': 'application/json'\n+  },\n+  body: JSON.stringify({ query: '...' })\n+});\n+```\n+\n+### ✅ Always Do This\n+\n+```typescript\n+// DO use the SDK\n+import { Valyu } from 'valyu-js';\n+\n+const valyu = new Valyu(process.env.VALYU_API_KEY);\n+const response = await valyu.search({ query: '...' });\n+```\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 1. Search API\n+\n+**Purpose:** Find information across web, academic, medical, transportation, financial, news, and proprietary sources.\n+\n+### When to Use\n+\n+- Finding recent information on any topic\n+- Academic research (arXiv, PubMed, bioRxiv, medRxiv)\n+- Financial data (SEC filings, earnings reports, stock data)\n+- News monitoring and current events\n+- Healthcare data (clinical trials, drug labels)\n+- Prediction markets (Polymarket, Kalshi)\n+- Transportation (UK National Rail, Global Shipping)\n+\n+### Basic Usage\n+\n+```typescript\n+const response = await valyu.search({\n+  query: \"transformer architecture attention mechanism 2024\",\n+  searchType: \"all\",\n+  maxNumResults: 10\n+});\n+```\n+\n+### Search Types\n+\n+| Type | Use For |\n+|------|---------|\n+| `all` | Everything - web, academic, financial, proprietary |\n+| `web` | General internet content only |\n+| `proprietary` | Licensed academic papers and research |\n+| `news` | News articles and current events |\n+\n+### Key Parameters\n+\n+| Parameter (TS/JS) | Parameter (Python) | Purpose | Example |\n+|-------------------|-------------------|---------|---------|\n+| `query` | `query` | Search query (under 400 chars) | `\"CRISPR gene editing 2024\"` |\n+| `searchType` | `search_type` | Source scope | `\"all\"`, `\"web\"`, `\"proprietary\"`, `\"news\"` |\n+| `maxNumResults` | `max_num_results` | Number of results (1-20) | `10` |\n+| `includedSources` | `included_sources` | Limit to specific sources | `[\"valyu/valyu-arxiv\", \"valyu/valyu-pubmed\"]` |\n+| `startDate` / `endDate` | `start_date` / `end_date` | Date filtering | `\"2024-01-01\"` |\n+| `relevanceThreshold` | `relevance_threshold` | Minimum relevance (0-1) | `0.7` |\n+\n+### Domain-Specific Search Patterns\n+\n+**Academic Research:**\n+```typescript\n+await valyu.search({\n+  query: \"CRISPR therapeutic applications clinical trials\",\n+  searchType: \"proprietary\",\n+  includedSources: [\"valyu/valyu-arxiv\", \"valyu/valyu-pubmed\", \"valyu/valyu-biorxiv\"],\n+  startDate: \"2024-01-01\"\n+});\n+```\n+\n+**Financial Analysis:**\n+```typescript\n+await valyu.search({\n+  query: \"Apple revenue Q4 2024 earnings\",\n+  searchType: \"all\",\n+  includedSources: [\"valyu/valyu-sec-filings\", \"valyu/valyu-earnings-US\"]\n+});\n+```\n+\n+**News Monitoring:**\n+```typescript\n+await valyu.search({\n+  query: \"AI regulation EU\",\n+  searchType: \"news\",\n+  startDate: \"2024-06-01\",\n+  countryCode: \"EU\"\n+});\n+```\n+\n+### Search Recipes\n+\n+For detailed patterns, see:\n+- [Basic Search Patterns](references/search-recipes/basic-search-all.md)\n+- [Academic Search](references/search-recipes/academic-search.md)\n+- [Finance Search](references/search-recipes/finance-search.md)\n+- [News Search](references/search-recipes/news-search.md)\n+- [Healthcare Search](references/search-recipes/healthcare-and-bio-search.md)\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 2. Contents API\n+\n+**Purpose:** Extract clean, structured content from web pages optimized for LLM processing.\n+\n+### When to Use\n+\n+- Converting web pages to clean markdown\n+- Extracting article text for summarization\n+- Parsing documentation for RAG systems\n+- Structured data extraction from product pages\n+- Processing academic papers\n+\n+### Basic Usage\n+\n+```typescript\n+const response = await valyu.contents({\n+  urls: [\"https://example.com/article\"]\n+});\n+```\n+\n+### With Summarization\n+\n+```typescript\n+const response = await valyu.contents({\n+  urls: [\"https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.12345\"],\n+  summary: \"Extract key findings in 3 bullet points\"\n+});\n+```\n+\n+### Structured Extraction (JSON Schema)\n+\n+```typescript\n+const response = await valyu.contents({\n+  urls: [\"https://example.com/product\"],\n+  summary: {\n+    type: \"object\",\n+    properties: {\n+      product_name: { type: \"string\" },\n+      price: { type: \"number\" },\n+      features: { type: \"array\", items: { type: \"string\" } }\n+    },\n+    required: [\"product_name\", \"price\"]\n+  }\n+});\n+```\n+\n+### Key Parameters\n+\n+| Parameter (TS/JS) | Parameter (Python) | Purpose | Example |\n+|-------------------|-------------------|---------|---------|\n+| `urls` | `urls` | URLs to process (1-10) | `[\"https://example.com\"]` |\n+| `responseLength` | `response_length` | Content length | `\"short\"`, `\"medium\"`, `\"large\"`, `\"max\"` |\n+| `extractEffort` | `extract_effort` | Extraction quality | `\"normal\"`, `\"high\"`, `\"auto\"` |\n+| `summary` | `summary` | AI summarization | `true`, `\"instructions\"`, or JSON schema |\n+| `screenshot` | `screenshot` | Capture screenshots | `true` |\n+\n+### Content Recipes\n+\n+For detailed patterns, see:\n+- [Basic Content Extraction](references/content-recipes/basic-content-extraction-from-web.md)\n+- [Extraction with Summary](references/content-recipes/basic-content-extraction-from-web-with-summary.md)\n+- [Structured Extraction](references/content-recipes/structured-content-extraction-from-web.md)\n+- [Research Paper Extraction](references/content-recipes/extract-content-from-research-paper.md)\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 3. Answer API\n+\n+**Purpose:** Get AI-powered answers grounded in real-time search results with citations.\n+\n+### When to Use\n+\n+- Questions requiring current information synthesis\n+- Multi-source fact verification\n+- Technical documentation questions\n+- Research requiring cited sources\n+- Structured data extraction from search results\n+\n+### Basic Usage\n+\n+```typescript\n+const response = await valyu.answer({\n+  query: \"What are the latest developments in quantum computing?\"\n+});\n+```\n+\n+### With Fast Mode (Lower Latency)\n+\n+```typescript\n+const response = await valyu.answer({\n+  query: \"Current Bitcoin price and 24h change\",\n+  fastMode: true\n+});\n+```\n+\n+### With Custom Instructions\n+\n+```typescript\n+const response = await valyu.answer({\n+  query: \"Compare React and Vue for enterprise applications\",\n+  systemInstructions: \"Provide a balanced comparison with pros and cons. Format as a comparison table.\"\n+});\n+```\n+\n+### With Streaming\n+\n+```typescript\n+const stream = await valyu.answer({\n+  query: \"Explain transformer architecture\",\n+  streaming: true\n+});\n+\n+for await (const chunk of stream) {\n+  // Handle: search_results, content, metadata, done, error\n+  console.log(chunk);\n+}\n+```\n+\n+### Structured Output\n+\n+```typescript\n+const response = await valyu.answer({\n+  query: \"Apple Q4 2024 financial highlights\",\n+  structuredOutput: {\n+    type: \"object\",\n+    properties: {\n+      revenue: { type: \"string\" },\n+      growthRate: { type: \"string\" },\n+      keyHighlights: { type: \"array\", items: { type: \"string\" } }\n+    }\n+  }\n+});\n+```\n+\n+### Key Parameters\n+\n+| Parameter (TS/JS) | Parameter (Python) | Purpose | Example |\n+|-------------------|-------------------|---------|---------|\n+| `query` | `query` | Question to answer | `\"What is quantum computing?\"` |\n+| `fastMode` | `fast_mode` | Lower latency | `true` |\n+| `systemInstructions` | `system_instructions` | AI directives | `\"Be concise\"` |\n+| `structuredOutput` | `structured_output` | JSON schema | `{type: \"object\", ...}` |\n+| `streaming` | `streaming` | Enable SSE streaming | `true` |\n+| `dataMaxPrice` | `data_max_price` | Dollar limit | `1.0` |\n+\n+### Answer Recipes\n+\n+For detailed patterns, see:\n+- [Basic Answer](references/answer-recipes/basic-answer.md)\n+- [Fast Mode](references/answer-recipes/answer-with-fast-mode.md)\n+- [Streaming](references/answer-recipes/answer-with-streaming.md)\n+- [Custom Instructions](references/answer-recipes/answer-with-custom-instructions.md)\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 4. DeepResearch API\n+\n+**Purpose:** Generate comprehensive research reports with detailed analysis and citations.\n+\n+### When to Use\n+\n+- Comprehensive market analysis\n+- Literature reviews\n+- Competitive intelligence\n+- Technical deep dives\n+- Topics requiring multi-source synthesis\n+\n+### Research Modes\n+\n+| Mode | Duration | Best For |\n+|------|----------|----------|\n+| `fast` | ~5 minutes | Quick lookups, simple questions |\n+| `standard` | ~10-20 minutes | Balanced research (most common) |\n+| `heavy` | ~90 minutes | Comprehensive analysis, complex topics |\n+\n+### Create Research Task\n+\n+```typescript\n+const task = await valyu.deepResearch.create({\n+  query: \"AI chip market competitive landscape 2024\",\n+  model: \"standard\"\n+});\n+// Returns: { deepresearch_id: \"abc123\", status: \"queued\" }\n+```\n+\n+### Poll for Completion\n+\n+```typescript\n+const status = await valyu.deepResearch.getStatus(task.deepresearch_id);\n+// status: \"queued\" | \"running\" | \"completed\" | \"failed\" | \"cancelled\"\n+\n+if (status.status === \"completed\") {\n+  console.log(status.output);  // Markdown report\n+  console.log(status.sources); // Cited sources\n+  console.log(status.pdf_url); // PDF download link\n+}\n+```\n+\n+### Key Parameters\n+\n+| Parameter (TS/JS) | Parameter (Python) | Purpose | Example |\n+|-------------------|-------------------|---------|---------|\n+| `query` | `query` | Research question | `\"AI market trends 2024\"` |\n+| `model` | `model` | Research depth | `\"fast\"`, `\"standard\"`, `\"heavy\"` |\n+| `outputFormat` | `output_format` | Report format | `\"markdown\"`, `\"pdf\"` |\n+| `includedSources` | `included_sources` | Source filtering | `[\"valyu/valyu-arxiv\", \"techcrunch.com\"]` |\n+| `startDate` / `endDate` | `start_date` / `end_date` | Date range | `\"2024-01-01\"` |\n+\n+### DeepResearch Recipes\n+\n+For detailed patterns, see:\n+- [Fast Research](references/deepresearch-recipes/create-a-fast-research-task-and-await-completion.md)\n+- [Standard Research](references/deepresearch-recipes/create-a-standard-research-task-and-await-completion.md)\n+- [Heavy Research](references/deepresearch-recipes/create-a-heavy-research-task-and-await-completion.md)\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 5. Query Writing Best Practices\n+\n+### Core Principles\n+\n+1. **Be specific** - Use domain terminology\n+2. **Be concise** - Keep queries under 400 characters\n+3. **Be focused** - One topic per query\n+4. **Add constraints** - Include timeframes, source types\n+\n+### Query Anatomy\n+\n+| Element | Description | Example |\n+|---------|-------------|---------|\n+| **Intent** | What you need | \"latest advancements\" vs \"overview\" |\n+| **Domain** | Topic terminology | \"transformer architecture\" |\n+| **Constraints** | Filters | \"2024\", \"peer-reviewed\" |\n+| **Source type** | Where to look | academic papers, SEC filings |\n+\n+### Good vs Bad Queries\n+\n+```\n+BAD:  \"I want to know about AI\"\n+GOOD: \"transformer attention mechanism survey 2024\"\n+\n+BAD:  \"Apple financial information\"\n+GOOD: \"Apple revenue growth Q4 2024 earnings SEC filing\"\n+\n+BAD:  \"gene editing research\"\n+GOOD: \"CRISPR off-target effects therapeutic applications 2024\"\n+```\n+\n+### Split Complex Requests\n+\n+```\n+# Don't do this\n+\"Tesla stock performance, new products, and Elon Musk statements\"\n+\n+# Do this instead\n+Query 1: \"Tesla stock performance Q4 2024\"\n+Query 2: \"Tesla Cybertruck production updates 2024\"\n+Query 3: \"Tesla FSD autonomous driving progress\"\n+```\n+\n+### Source Filtering\n+\n+Use `includedSources` for domain authority:\n+​\n+Financial Research Collection. Some sources to include:\n+- `valyu/valyu-sec-filings` - SEC regulatory filings\n+- `valyu/valyu-stocks` - Stock market data\n+- `valyu/valyu-earnings-US` - Earnings reports\n+- `reuters.com` - Financial news\n+- `bloomberg.com` - Market analysis\n+​\n+Medical Research Collection. Some sources to include:\n+- `valyu/valyu-pubmed` - Medical literature\n+- `valyu/valyu-clinical-trials` - Clinical trial data\n+- `valyu/valyu-drug-labels` - FDA drug information\n+- `nejm.org` - New England Journal of Medicine\n+- `thelancet.com` - The Lancet\n+​\n+Tech Documentation Collection. Some sources to include:\n+- `docs.aws.amazon.com` - AWS documentation\n+- `cloud.google.com/docs` - Google Cloud docs\n+- `learn.microsoft.com` - Microsoft docs\n+- `kubernetes.io/docs` - Kubernetes docs\n+- `developer.mozilla.org` - MDN Web Docs\n+\n+```javascript\n+// Academic\n+includedSources: [\"valyu/valyu-arxiv\", \"valyu/valyu-pubmed\", \"nature\"]\n+\n+// Financial\n+includedSources: [\"valyu/valyu-sec-filings\", \"bloomberg.com\", \"reuters.com\"]\n+\n+// Tech news\n+includedSources: [\"techcrunch.com\", \"theverge.com\", \"arstechnica.com\"]\n+```\n+\n+For complete prompting guide, see [references/prompting.md](references/prompting.md).\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 6. Common Workflows\n+\n+### Research Workflow\n+\n+```typescript\n+// 1. Quick search to find sources\n+const searchResults = await valyu.search({\n+  query: \"CRISPR therapeutic applications\",\n+  searchType: \"proprietary\",\n+  maxNumResults: 20\n+});\n+\n+// 2. Extract key content from top results\n+const contents = await valyu.contents({\n+  urls: searchResults.results.slice(0, 3).map(r => r.url),\n+  summary: \"Extract key findings\"\n+});\n+\n+// 3. Deep analysis for comprehensive report\n+const research = await valyu.deepResearch.create({\n+  query: \"CRISPR therapeutic applications comprehensive review\",\n+  model: \"heavy\"\n+});\n+```\n+\n+### Financial Analysis Workflow\n+\n+```typescript\n+// 1. Get SEC filings\n+const filings = await valyu.search({\n+  query: \"Apple 10-K 2024\",\n+  includedSources: [\"valyu/valyu-sec-filings\"]\n+});\n+\n+// 2. Quick synthesis\n+const summary = await valyu.answer({\n+  query: \"Apple Q4 2024 financial highlights\",\n+  fastMode: true\n+});\n+\n+// 3. Structured extraction\n+const metrics = await valyu.answer({\n+  query: \"Apple financial metrics 2024\",\n+  structuredOutput: {\n+    type: \"object\",\n+    properties: {\n+      revenue: { type: \"string\" },\n+      netIncome: { type: \"string\" },\n+      growthRate: { type: \"string\" }\n+    }\n+  }\n+});\n+```\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 7. Available Data Sources\n+\n+Valyu provides access to 25+ specialized datasets:\n+\n+| Category | Examples |\n+|----------|----------|\n+| **Academic** | arXiv (2.5M+ papers), PubMed (37M+), bioRxiv, medRxiv |\n+| **Financial** | SEC filings, earnings transcripts, stock data, crypto |\n+| **Healthcare** | Clinical trials, DailyMed, PubChem, drug labels, ChEMBL, DrugBank, Open Target, WHO ICD |\n+| **Economic** | FRED, BLS, World Bank, US Treasury, Destatis |\n+| **Predictions** | Polymarket, Kalshi |\n+| **Patents** | US patent database |\n+| **Transportation** | UK Rail, Ship Tracking |\n+\n+For complete datasource reference, see [references/datasources.md](references/datasources.md).\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 8. API Reference\n+\n+For complete API documentation including all parameters, response structures, and error codes, see [references/api-guide.md](references/api-guide.md).\n+\n+---\n+\n+## 9. Integration Guides\n+\n+Platform-specific integration documentation:\n+\n+- [Anthropic Claude](references/integrations/anthropic.md)\n+- [OpenAI](references/integrations/openai.md)\n+- [Vercel AI SDK](references/integrations/vercel-ai-sdk.md)\n+- [LangChain](references/integrations/langchain.md)\n+- [LlamaIndex](references/integrations/llamaindex.md)\n+- [MCP Server](references/integrations/mcp-server.md)\n+\n+---\n+\n+## Additional Resources\n+\n+- [All Recipes Index](references/recipes.md)\n+- [Design Philosophy](references/design-philosophy.md)\n+- [Prompting Guide](references/prompting.md)\n+- [Full API Reference](references/api-guide.md)\ndiff --git a/.agents/skills/valyu-best-practices/references/answer-recipes/answer-with-custom-instructions.md b/.agents/skills/valyu-best-practices/references/answer-recipes/answer-with-custom-instructions.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..ecd114d\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/.agents/skills/valyu-best-practices/references/answer-recipes/answer-with-custom-instructions.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,38 @@\n+# Answer with Custom Instructions\n+\n+Guide the AI on how to process and format the answer using `systemInstructions`.\n+\n+```typescript\n+import { Valyu } from \"valyu-js\";\n+\n+const valyu = new Valyu(YOUR_VALYU_API_KEY_HERE);\n+\n+const data = await valyu.answer({\n+  query: \"climate change research\",\n+  systemInstructions: \"Focus on practical applications and commercial impact. Summarise key findings as bullet points.\"\n+});\n+\n+console.log(data.contents);\n+```\n+\n+```python\n+from valyu import Valyu\n+\n+valyu = Valyu()\n+\n+data = valyu.answer(\n+    query=\"climate change research\",\n+    system_instructions=\"Focus on practical applications and commercial impact. Summarise key findings as bullet points.\"\n+)\n+\n+print(data[\"contents\"])\n+```\n+\n+## Example Instructions\n+\n+- \"Respond in bullet points\"\n+- \"Focus on technical details\"\n+- \"Summarize in 2-3 paragraphs\"\n+- \"Include specific numbers and statistics\"\n+- \"Write for a non-technical audience\"\n+- \"Compare and contrast the options\"\ndiff --git a/.agents/skills/valyu-best-practices/references/answer-recipes/answer-with-streaming.md b/.agents/skills/valyu-best-practices/references/answer-recipes/answer-with-streaming.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..8779c5c\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/.agents/skills/valyu-best-practices/references/answer-recipes/answer-with-streaming.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@\n+# Answer with Streaming\n+\n+Enable streaming for progressive answer generation.\n+\n+The stream sends data in sequence: search results first, then content chunks, then metadata.\n+\n+```typescript\n+import { Valyu } from \"valyu-js\";\n+\n+const valyu = new Valyu(YOUR_VALYU_API_KEY_HERE);\n+\n+const stream = await valyu.answer({\n+  query: \"Explain the implications of recent AI regulation\",\n+  streaming: true\n+});\n+\n+for await (const chunk of stream) {\n+  switch (chunk.type) {\n+    case 'search_results':\n+      console.log('Sources found:', chunk.data.length);\n+      break;\n+    case 'content':\n+      process.stdout.write(chunk.data);\n+      break;\n+    case 'metadata':\n+      console.log('\\nCost:', chunk.data.cost);\n+      break;\n+    case 'done':\n+      console.log('\\nComplete');\n+      break;\n+    case 'error':\n+      console.error('Error:', chunk.data);\n+      break;\n+  }\n+}\n+```\n+\n+## Chunk Types\n+\n+| Type | Description |\n+|------|-------------|\n+| `search_results` | Found sources |\n+| `content` | Answer text chunks |\n+| `metadata` | Cost and usage info |\n+| `done` | Completion signal |\n+| `error` | Error information |\ndiff --git a/.agents/skills/valyu-best-practices/references/answer-recipes/basic-answer.md b/.agents/skills/valyu-best-practices/references/answer-recipes/basic-answer.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..91e641c\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/.agents/skills/valyu-best-practices/references/answer-recipes/basic-answer.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,32 @@\n+# Basic Answer\n+\n+AI-powered answers with real-time search. Searches across web, academic, and financial sources, then uses AI to generate a readable response.\n+\n+```typescript\n+import { Valyu } from \"valyu-js\";\n+\n+const valyu = new Valyu(YOUR_VALYU_API_KEY_HERE);\n+\n+const data = await valyu.answer({\n+  query: \"latest developments in quantum computing\",\n+});\n+\n+console.log(data.contents);\n+```\n+\n+```python\n+from valyu import Valyu\n+\n+valyu = Valyu()  # Uses VALYU_API_KEY from env\n+\n+data = valyu.answer(\n+    query=\"latest developments in quantum computing\",\n+)\n+\n+print(data[\"contents\"])\n+```\n+\n+## CLI\n+```bash\n+scripts/valyu answer \"What are the latest developments in quantum computing?\"\n+```\ndiff --git a/.agents/skills/valyu-best-practices/references/api-guide.md b/.agents/skills/valyu-best-practices/references/api-guide.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..846ad0b\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/.agents/skills/valyu-best-practices/references/api-guide.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,437 @@\n+# Valyu API Reference Guide\n+\n+Complete API documentation for all Valyu endpoints. Use this reference when implementing searches, content extraction, AI-powered answers, or deep research.\n+\n+## API Overview\n+\n+| Endpoint | Purpose | Best For |\n+|----------|---------|----------|\n+| `/v1/search` | Multi-source search | Finding information across web, academic, financial sources |\n+| `/v1/contents` | URL content extraction | Clean markdown from web pages, PDFs |\n+| `/v1/answer` | AI-powered answers | Questions requiring synthesis from multiple sources |\n+| `/v1/deepresearch` | Comprehensive research | In-depth reports with citations |\n+| `/v1/datasources` | Discover available data sources | Dynamic tool discovery, cost estimation |\n+\n+## Authentication\n+\n+All endpoints require the `x-api-key` header:\n+```\n+x-api-key: your_valyu_api_key\n+```\n+\n+Get your API key at https://platform.valyu.ai ($10 free credits).\n+\n+---\n+\n+## Search API (`POST /v1/search`)\n+\n+Real-time search across web, academic, financial, economic, medical research, news, patents, prediction markets, transportation and proprietary data sources.\n+\n+### Parameters\n+\n+| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |\n+|-----------|------|---------|-------------|\n+| `query` | string | **required** | Search query (recommended: under 400 chars) |\n+| `search_type` | enum | `\"all\"` | `\"all\"`, `\"web\"`, `\"proprietary\"`, `\"news\"` |\n+| `max_num_results` | int | 10 | 1-20 (up to 100 with enhanced access) |\n+| `fast_mode` | bool | false | Faster responses, shorter results |\n+| `relevance_threshold` | float | 0.5 | Minimum relevance score (0.0-1.0) |\n+| `response_length` | enum | `\"medium\"` | `\"short\"` (25k), `\"medium\"` (50k), `\"large\"` (100k), `\"max\"` |\n+| `included_sources` | array | [] | Domains/datasets to include |\n+| `excluded_sources` | array | [] | Domains/datasets to exclude |\n+| `start_date` | string | null | Filter from date (YYYY-MM-DD) |\n+| `end_date` | string | null | Filter until date (YYYY-MM-DD) |\n+| `country_code` | string | null | 2-letter ISO code for geographic bias |\n+\n+### Search Types Explained\n+\n+- **`all`**: Searches everything - web, academic papers, financial data,economic, medical research, news, patents, prediction markets, transportation,proprietary sources\n+- **`web`**: General internet content only\n+- **`proprietary`**: Licensed academic papers, research, books\n+- **`news`**: News articles and current events\n+\n+### Response Structure\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"success\": true,\n+  \"tx_id\": \"tx_abc123\",\n+  \"results\": [\n+    {\n+      \"title\": \"Article Title\",\n+      \"url\": \"https://example.com/article\",\n+      \"content\": \"Full extracted content in markdown...\",\n+      \"source\": \"web\",\n+      \"relevance_score\": 0.92,\n+      \"publication_date\": \"2024-01-15\",\n+      \"length\": 5420,\n+      \"price\": 0.002\n+    }\n+  ],\n+  \"total_results\": 10,\n+  \"total_deduction_dollars\": 0.025,\n+  \"total_characters\": 54200\n+}\n+```\n+\n+### When to Use Search\n+\n+- Finding recent information on any topic\n+- Academic research across arXiv, PubMed, bioRxiv\n+- Financial data from SEC filings, earnings reports\n+- News monitoring and current events\n+- Domain-specific searches with source filtering\n+\n+---\n+\n+## Contents API (`POST /v1/contents`)\n+\n+Extract clean, structured content from web pages optimized for LLM processing.\n+\n+### Parameters\n+\n+| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |\n+|-----------|------|---------|-------------|\n+| `urls` | array | **required** | 1-10 URLs to process |\n+| `response_length` | enum/int | `\"short\"` | `\"short\"` (25k), `\"medium\"` (50k), `\"large\"` (100k), `\"max\"`, or custom int |\n+| `extract_effort` | enum | `\"normal\"` | `\"normal\"`, `\"high\"`, `\"auto\"` |\n+| `screenshot` | bool | false | Capture page screenshots |\n+| `summary` | bool/string/object | false | AI summarization options |\n+\n+### Summary Options\n+\n+```javascript\n+// Simple summary\n+summary: true\n+\n+// Custom instructions\n+summary: \"Extract key findings in bullet points\"\n+\n+// Structured extraction with JSON schema\n+summary: {\n+  type: \"object\",\n+  properties: {\n+    product_name: { type: \"string\" },\n+    price: { type: \"number\" },\n+    features: { type: \"array\", items: { type: \"string\" } }\n+  },\n+  required: [\"product_name\", \"price\"]\n+}\n+```\n+\n+### Response Structure\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"success\": true,\n+  \"tx_id\": \"tx_xyz789\",\n+  \"results\": [\n+    {\n+      \"title\": \"Page Title\",\n+      \"url\": \"https://example.com\",\n+      \"content\": \"Clean markdown content...\",\n+      \"description\": \"Meta description\",\n+      \"data_type\": \"unstructured\",\n+      \"length\": 12840,\n+      \"price\": 0.002\n+    }\n+  ],\n+  \"urls_requested\": 1,\n+  \"urls_processed\": 1,\n+  \"urls_failed\": 0,\n+  \"total_cost_dollars\": 0.002\n+}\n+```\n+\n+### When to Use Contents\n+\n+- Extracting article text for summarization\n+- Parsing documentation for RAG systems\n+- Cleaning web pages before LLM processing\n+- Batch URL-to-text conversion\n+- Structured data extraction from product pages\n+\n+---\n+\n+## Answer API (`POST /v1/answer`)\n+\n+AI-powered answers grounded in real-time search results with citations.\n+\n+### Parameters\n+\n+| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |\n+|-----------|------|---------|-------------|\n+| `query` | string | **required** | Question to answer |\n+| `search_type` | enum | `\"all\"` | Source scope |\n+| `fast_mode` | bool | false | Lower latency, shorter results |\n+| `system_instructions` | string | null | Custom AI directives (max 2000 chars) |\n+| `structured_output` | object | null | JSON schema for formatted responses |\n+| `streaming` | bool | false | Enable SSE streaming |\n+| `data_max_price` | float | 1 | Dollar limit for search data |\n+| `included_sources` | array | [] | Domains to prioritize |\n+| `excluded_sources` | array | [] | Domains to exclude |\n+| `start_date` | string | null | Filter results from date |\n+| `end_date` | string | null | Filter results until date |\n+| `country_code` | string | null | Geographic bias |\n+\n+### Structured Output Example\n+\n+```javascript\n+structured_output: {\n+  type: \"object\",\n+  properties: {\n+    summary: { type: \"string\" },\n+    key_points: { type: \"array\", items: { type: \"string\" } },\n+    confidence: { type: \"number\" }\n+  }\n+}\n+```\n+\n+### Response Structure\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"success\": true,\n+  \"tx_id\": \"tx_answer123\",\n+  \"original_query\": \"What is quantum computing?\",\n+  \"contents\": \"Quantum computing is a type of computation...\",\n+  \"data_type\": \"unstructured\",\n+  \"search_results\": [...],\n+  \"search_metadata\": {\n+    \"number_of_results\": 8,\n+    \"total_characters\": 45000\n+  },\n+  \"cost\": {\n+    \"total_deduction_dollars\": 0.045,\n+    \"search_deduction_dollars\": 0.025,\n+    \"ai_deduction_dollars\": 0.020\n+  }\n+}\n+```\n+\n+### When to Use Answer\n+\n+- Questions requiring current information synthesis\n+- Multi-source fact verification\n+- Technical documentation questions\n+- Research requiring cited sources\n+- Structured data extraction from search results\n+\n+---\n+\n+## DeepResearch API\n+\n+Async comprehensive research with detailed reports and citations.\n+\n+### Create Task (`POST /v1/deepresearch/tasks`)\n+\n+| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |\n+|-----------|------|---------|-------------|\n+| `query` | string | **required** | Research question |\n+| `model` | enum | `\"standard\"` | `\"fast\"` (~5 min), `\"standard\"` (~10-20 min), `\"heavy\"` (~90 min) |\n+| `output_format` | enum | `\"markdown\"` | `\"markdown\"`, `\"pdf\"`, or JSON schema |\n+| `included_sources` | array | [] | Sources to prioritize |\n+| `excluded_sources` | array | [] | Sources to exclude |\n+| `start_date` | string | null | Filter from date |\n+| `end_date` | string | null | Filter until date |\n+\n+### Research Modes\n+\n+| Mode | Duration | Best For |\n+|------|----------|----------|\n+| `fast` | ~5 minutes | Quick lookups, simple questions |\n+| `standard` | ~10-20 minutes | Balanced research, most use Also add it to the project settings."},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":658,"cache_read_input_tokens":86618,"output_tokens":94,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":658,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"not_available","iterations":[{"input_tokens":3,"output_tokens":94,"cache_read_input_tokens":86618,"cache_creation_input_tokens":658,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":658},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"},"diagnostics":null},"requestId":"req_011CbnX7u2gVeYtqb8PnFgew","type":"assistant","uuid":"639474bb-8615-427b-928d-3262edd1f79e","timestamp":"2026-06-06T19:00:03.572Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור","sessionId":"368947d7-a64b-4fc9-a2f6-fccfabe83369","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"master"}

{"parentUuid":"b9c1063d-8018-4351-9c2d-a2615a7c5ad6","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"9e618a17-1618-45de-8339-c6425b3260b2","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"# Update Config Skill\n\nModify Claude Code configuration by updating settings.json files.\n\n## When Hooks Are Required (Not Memory)\n\nIf the user wants something to happen automatically in response to an EVENT, they need a **hook** configured in settings.json. Memory/preferences cannot trigger automated actions.\n\n**These require hooks:**\n- \"Before compacting, ask me what to preserve\" → PreCompact hook\n- \"After writing files, run prettier\" → PostToolUse hook with Write|Edit matcher\n- \"When I run bash commands, log them\" → PreToolUse hook with Bash matcher\n- \"Always run tests after code changes\" → PostToolUse hook\n\n**Hook events:** PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PreCompact, PostCompact, Stop, Notification, SessionStart\n\n## CRITICAL: Read Before Write\n\n**Always read the existing settings file before making changes.** Merge new settings with existing ones - never replace the entire file.\n\n## CRITICAL: Use AskUserQuestion for Ambiguity\n\nWhen the user's request is ambiguous, use AskUserQuestion to clarify:\n- Which settings file to modify (user/project/local)\n- Whether to add to existing arrays or replace them\n- Specific values when multiple options exist\n\n## Decision: /config command vs Direct Edit\n\n**Suggest the `/config` slash command** for these simple settings:\n- `theme`, `editorMode`, `verbose`, `model`\n- `language`, `alwaysThinkingEnabled`\n- `permissions.defaultMode`\n\n**Edit settings.json directly** for:\n- Hooks (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, etc.)\n- Complex permission rules (allow/deny arrays)\n- Environment variables\n- MCP server configuration\n- Plugin configuration\n\n## Workflow\n\n1. **Clarify intent** - Ask if the request is ambiguous\n2. **Read existing file** - Use Read tool on the target settings file\n3. **Merge carefully** - Preserve existing settings, especially arrays\n4. **Edit file** - Use Edit tool (if file doesn't exist, ask user to create it first)\n5. **Confirm** - Tell user what was changed\n\n## Merging Arrays (Important!)\n\nWhen adding to permission arrays or hook arrays, **merge with existing**, don't replace:\n\n**WRONG** (replaces existing permissions):\n```json\n{ \"permissions\": { \"allow\": [\"Bash(npm *)\"] } }\n```\n\n**RIGHT** (preserves existing + adds new):\n```json\n{\n  \"permissions\": {\n    \"allow\": [\n      \"Bash(git *)\",      // existing\n      \"Edit(.claude)\",    // existing\n      \"Bash(npm *)\"       // new\n    ]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n## Settings File Locations\n\nChoose the appropriate file based on scope:\n\n| File | Scope | Git | Use For |\n|------|-------|-----|---------|\n| `~/.claude/settings.json` | Global | N/A | Personal preferences for all projects |\n| `.claude/settings.json` | Project | Commit | Team-wide hooks, permissions, plugins |\n| `.claude/settings.local.json` | Project | Gitignore | Personal overrides for this project |\n\nSettings load in order: user → project → local (later overrides earlier).\n\n## Settings Schema Reference\n\n### Permissions\n```json\n{\n  \"permissions\": {\n    \"allow\": [\"Bash(npm *)\", \"Edit(.claude)\", \"Read\"],\n    \"deny\": [\"Bash(rm -rf *)\"],\n    \"ask\": [\"Write(/etc/*)\"],\n    \"defaultMode\": \"default\" | \"plan\" | \"acceptEdits\" | \"dontAsk\",\n    \"additionalDirectories\": [\"/extra/dir\"]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Permission Rule Syntax:**\n- Exact match: `\"Bash(npm run test)\"`\n- Prefix wildcard: `\"Bash(git *)\"` - matches `git`, `git status`, `git commit`, etc.\n- Tool only: `\"Read\"` - allows all Read operations\n\n### Environment Variables\n```json\n{\n  \"env\": {\n    \"DEBUG\": \"true\",\n    \"MY_API_KEY\": \"value\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Model & Agent\n```json\n{\n  \"model\": \"sonnet\",  // or \"opus\", \"haiku\", full model ID\n  \"agent\": \"agent-name\",\n  \"alwaysThinkingEnabled\": true\n}\n```\n\n### Attribution (Commits & PRs)\n```json\n{\n  \"attribution\": {\n    \"commit\": \"Custom commit trailer text\",\n    \"pr\": \"Custom PR description text\"\n  }\n}\n```\nSet `commit` or `pr` to empty string `\"\"` to hide that attribution.\n\n### MCP Server Management\n```json\n{\n  \"enableAllProjectMcpServers\": true,\n  \"enabledMcpjsonServers\": [\"server1\", \"server2\"],\n  \"disabledMcpjsonServers\": [\"blocked-server\"]\n}\n```\n\n### Plugins\n```json\n{\n  \"enabledPlugins\": {\n    \"formatter@anthropic-tools\": true\n  }\n}\n```\nPlugin syntax: `plugin-name@source` where source is `claude-code-marketplace`, `claude-plugins-official`, or `builtin`.\n\n### Other Settings\n- `language`: Preferred response language (e.g., \"japanese\")\n- `cleanupPeriodDays`: Days to keep transcripts before automatic cleanup (default: 30; minimum 1)\n- `respectGitignore`: Whether to respect .gitignore (default: true)\n- `spinnerTipsEnabled`: Show tips in spinner\n- `spinnerVerbs`: Customize spinner verbs (`{ \"mode\": \"append\" | \"replace\", \"verbs\": [...] }`)\n- `spinnerTipsOverride`: Override spinner tips (`{ \"excludeDefault\": true, \"tips\": [\"Custom tip\"] }`)\n- `syntaxHighlightingDisabled`: Disable diff highlighting\n\n\n## Hooks Configuration\n\nHooks run commands at specific points in Claude Code's lifecycle.\n\n### Hook Structure\n```json\n{\n  \"hooks\": {\n    \"EVENT_NAME\": [\n      {\n        \"matcher\": \"ToolName|OtherTool\",\n        \"hooks\": [\n          {\n            \"type\": \"command\",\n            \"command\": \"your-command-here\",\n            \"timeout\": 60,\n            \"statusMessage\": \"Running...\"\n          }\n        ]\n      }\n    ]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Hook Events\n\n| Event | Matcher | Purpose |\n|-------|---------|---------|\n| PermissionRequest | Tool name | Run before permission prompt |\n| PreToolUse | Tool name | Run before tool, can block |\n| PostToolUse | Tool name | Run after successful tool |\n| PostToolUseFailure | Tool name | Run after tool fails |\n| Notification | Notification type | Run on notifications |\n| Stop | - | Run when Claude stops (including clear, resume, compact) |\n| PreCompact | \"manual\"/\"auto\" | Before compaction |\n| PostCompact | \"manual\"/\"auto\" | After compaction (receives summary) |\n| UserPromptSubmit | - | When user submits |\n| SessionStart | - | When session starts |\n\n**Common tool matchers:** `Bash`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Read`, `Glob`, `Grep`\n\n### Hook Types\n\n**1. Command Hook** - Runs a shell command:\n```json\n{ \"type\": \"command\", \"command\": \"prettier --write $FILE\", \"timeout\": 30 }\n```\n\n**2. Prompt Hook** - Evaluates a condition with LLM:\n```json\n{ \"type\": \"prompt\", \"prompt\": \"Is this safe? $ARGUMENTS\" }\n```\nOnly available for tool events: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PermissionRequest.\n\n**3. Agent Hook** - Runs an agent with tools:\n```json\n{ \"type\": \"agent\", \"prompt\": \"Verify tests pass: $ARGUMENTS\" }\n```\nOnly available for tool events: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PermissionRequest.\n\n### Hook Input (stdin JSON)\n```json\n{\n  \"session_id\": \"abc123\",\n  \"tool_name\": \"Write\",\n  \"tool_input\": { \"file_path\": \"/path/to/file.txt\", \"content\": \"...\" },\n  \"tool_response\": { \"success\": true }  // PostToolUse only\n}\n```\n\n### Hook JSON Output\n\nHooks can return JSON to control behavior:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"systemMessage\": \"Warning shown to user in UI\",\n  \"continue\": false,\n  \"stopReason\": \"Message shown when blocking\",\n  \"suppressOutput\": false,\n  \"decision\": \"block\",\n  \"reason\": \"Explanation for decision\",\n  \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n    \"hookEventName\": \"PostToolUse\",\n    \"additionalContext\": \"Context injected back to model\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Fields:**\n- `systemMessage` - Display a message to the user (all hooks)\n- `continue` - Set to `false` to block/stop (default: true)\n- `stopReason` - Message shown when `continue` is false\n- `suppressOutput` - Hide stdout from transcript (default: false)\n- `decision` - \"block\" for PostToolUse/Stop/UserPromptSubmit hooks (deprecated for PreToolUse, use hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision instead)\n- `reason` - Explanation for decision\n- `hookSpecificOutput` - Event-specific output (must include `hookEventName`):\n  - `additionalContext` - Text injected into model context\n  - `permissionDecision` - \"allow\", \"deny\", or \"ask\" (PreToolUse only)\n  - `permissionDecisionReason` - Reason for the permission decision (PreToolUse only)\n  - `updatedInput` - Modified tool input (PreToolUse only)\n\n### Common Patterns\n\n**Auto-format after writes:**\n```json\n{\n  \"hooks\": {\n    \"PostToolUse\": [{\n      \"matcher\": \"Write|Edit\",\n      \"hooks\": [{\n        \"type\": \"command\",\n        \"command\": \"jq -r '.tool_response.filePath // .tool_input.file_path' | { read -r f; prettier --write \\\"$f\\\"; } 2>/dev/null || true\"\n      }]\n    }]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Log all bash commands:**\n```json\n{\n  \"hooks\": {\n    \"PreToolUse\": [{\n      \"matcher\": \"Bash\",\n      \"hooks\": [{\n        \"type\": \"command\",\n        \"command\": \"jq -r '.tool_input.command' >> ~/.claude/bash-log.txt\"\n      }]\n    }]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Stop hook that displays message to user:**\n\nCommand must output JSON with `systemMessage` field:\n```bash\n# Example command that outputs: {\"systemMessage\": \"Session complete!\"}\necho '{\"systemMessage\": \"Session complete!\"}'\n```\n\n**Run tests after code changes:**\n```json\n{\n  \"hooks\": {\n    \"PostToolUse\": [{\n      \"matcher\": \"Write|Edit\",\n      \"hooks\": [{\n        \"type\": \"command\",\n        \"command\": \"jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_response.filePath' | grep -E '\\\\.(ts|js)$' && npm test || true\"\n      }]\n    }]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n\n## Constructing a Hook (with verification)\n\nGiven an event, matcher, target file, and desired behavior, follow this flow. Each step catches a different failure class — a hook that silently does nothing is worse than no hook.\n\n1. **Dedup check.** Read the target file. If a hook already exists on the same event+matcher, show the existing command and ask: keep it, replace it, or add alongside.\n\n2. **Construct the command for THIS project — don't assume.** The hook receives JSON on stdin. Build a command that:\n   - Extracts any needed payload safely — use `jq -r` into a quoted variable or `{ read -r f; ... \"$f\"; }`, NOT unquoted `| xargs` (splits on spaces)\n   - Invokes the underlying tool the way this project runs it (npx/bunx/yarn/pnpm? Makefile target? globally-installed?)\n   - Skips inputs the tool doesn't handle (formatters often have `--ignore-unknown`; if not, guard by extension)\n   - Stays RAW for now — no `|| true`, no stderr suppression. You'll wrap it after the pipe-test passes.\n\n3. **Pipe-test the raw command.** Synthesize the stdin payload the hook will receive and pipe it directly:\n   - `Pre|PostToolUse` on `Write|Edit`: `echo '{\"tool_name\":\"Edit\",\"tool_input\":{\"file_path\":\"<a real file from this repo>\"}}' | <cmd>`\n   - `Pre|PostToolUse` on `Bash`: `echo '{\"tool_name\":\"Bash\",\"tool_input\":{\"command\":\"ls\"}}' | <cmd>`\n   - `Stop`/`UserPromptSubmit`/`SessionStart`: most commands don't read stdin, so `echo '{}' | <cmd>` suffices\n\n   Check exit code AND side effect (file actually formatted, test actually ran). If it fails you get a real error — fix (wrong package manager? tool not installed? jq path wrong?) and retest. Once it works, wrap with `2>/dev/null || true` (unless the user wants a blocking check).\n\n4. **Write the JSON.** Merge into the target file (schema shape in the \"Hook Structure\" section above). If this creates `.claude/settings.local.json` for the first time, add it to .gitignore — the Write tool doesn't auto-gitignore it.\n\n5. **Validate syntax + schema in one shot:**\n\n   `jq -e '.hooks.<event>[] | select(.matcher == \"<matcher>\") | .hooks[] | select(.type == \"command\") | .command' <target-file>`\n\n   Exit 0 + prints your command = correct. Exit 4 = matcher doesn't match. Exit 5 = malformed JSON or wrong nesting. A broken settings.json silently disables ALL settings from that file — fix any pre-existing malformation too.\n\n6. **Prove the hook fires** — only for `Pre|PostToolUse` on a matcher you can trigger in-turn (`Write|Edit` via Edit, `Bash` via Bash). `Stop`/`UserPromptSubmit`/`SessionStart` fire outside this turn — skip to step 7.\n\n   For a **formatter** on `PostToolUse`/`Write|Edit`: introduce a detectable violation via Edit (two consecutive blank lines, bad indentation, missing semicolon — something this formatter corrects; NOT trailing whitespace, Edit strips that before writing), re-read, confirm the hook **fixed** it. For **anything else**: temporarily prefix the command in settings.json with `echo \"$(date) hook fired\" >> /tmp/claude-hook-check.txt; `, trigger the matching tool (Edit for `Write|Edit`, a harmless `true` for `Bash`), read the sentinel file.\n\n   **Always clean up** — revert the violation, strip the sentinel prefix — whether the proof passed or failed.\n\n   **If proof fails but pipe-test passed and `jq -e` passed**: the settings watcher isn't watching `.claude/` — it only watches directories that had a settings file when this session started. The hook is written correctly. Tell the user to open `/hooks` once (reloads config) or restart — you can't do this yourself; `/hooks` is a user UI menu and opening it ends this turn.\n\n7. **Handoff.** Tell the user the hook is live (or needs `/hooks`/restart per the watcher caveat). Point them at `/hooks` to review, edit, or disable it later. The UI only shows \"Ran N hooks\" if a hook errors or is slow — silent success is invisible by design.\n\n\n## Example Workflows\n\n### Adding a Hook\n\nUser: \"Format my code after Claude writes it\"\n\n1. **Clarify**: Which formatter? (prettier, gofmt, etc.)\n2. **Read**: `.claude/settings.json` (or create if missing)\n3. **Merge**: Add to existing hooks, don't replace\n4. **Result**:\n```json\n{\n  \"hooks\": {\n    \"PostToolUse\": [{\n      \"matcher\": \"Write|Edit\",\n      \"hooks\": [{\n        \"type\": \"command\",\n        \"command\": \"jq -r '.tool_response.filePath // .tool_input.file_path' | { read -r f; prettier --write \\\"$f\\\"; } 2>/dev/null || true\"\n      }]\n    }]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Adding Permissions\n\nUser: \"Allow npm commands without prompting\"\n\n1. **Read**: Existing permissions\n2. **Merge**: Add `Bash(npm *)` to allow array\n3. **Result**: Combined with existing allows\n\n### Environment Variables\n\nUser: \"Set DEBUG=true\"\n\n1. **Decide**: User settings (global) or project settings?\n2. **Read**: Target file\n3. **Merge**: Add to env object\n```json\n{ \"env\": { \"DEBUG\": \"true\" } }\n```\n\n## Common Mistakes to Avoid\n\n1. **Replacing instead of merging** - Always preserve existing settings\n2. **Wrong file** - Ask user if scope is unclear\n3. **Invalid JSON** - Validate syntax after changes\n4. **Forgetting to read first** - Always read before write\n\n## Troubleshooting Hooks\n\nIf a hook isn't running:\n1. **Check the settings file** - Read ~/.claude/settings.json or .claude/settings.json\n2. **Verify JSON syntax** - Invalid JSON silently fails\n3. **Check the matcher** - Does it match the tool name? (e.g., \"Bash\", \"Write\", \"Edit\")\n4. **Check hook type** - Is it \"command\", \"prompt\", or \"agent\"?\n5. **Test the command** - Run the hook command manually to see if it works\n6. **Use --debug** - Run `claude --debug` to see hook execution logs\n\n\n## Full Settings JSON Schema\n\n```json\n{\n  \"$schema\": \"https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema\",\n  \"type\": \"object\",\n  \"properties\": {\n    \"$schema\": {\n      \"description\": \"JSON Schema reference for Claude Code settings\",\n      \"type\": \"string\",\n      \"const\": \"https://json.schemastore.org/claude-code-settings.json\"\n    },\n    \"apiKeyHelper\": {\n      \"description\": \"Path to a script that outputs authentication values\",\n      \"type\": \"string\"\n    },\n    \"proxyAuthHelper\": {\n      \"description\": \"Shell command that outputs a Proxy-Authorization header value (EAP)\",\n      \"type\": \"string\"\n    },\n    \"awsCredentialExport\": {\n      \"description\": \"Path to a script that exports AWS credentials\",\n      \"type\": \"string\"\n    },\n    \"awsAuthRefresh\": {\n      \"description\": \"Path to a script that refreshes AWS authentication\",\n      \"type\": \"string\"\n    },\n    \"gcpAuthRefresh\": {\n      \"description\": \"Command to refresh GCP authentication (e.g., gcloud auth application-default login)\",\n      \"type\": \"string\"\n    },\n    \"policyHelper\": {\n      \"description\": \"Executable that computes managed settings at startup. Honored only from admin-controlled policy sources.\",\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"path\": {\n          \"description\": \"Absolute path to the helper executable\",\n          \"type\": \"string\"\n        },\n        \"timeoutMs\": {\n          \"type\": \"integer\",\n          \"minimum\": 1000,\n          \"maximum\": 9007199254740991\n        },\n        \"refreshIntervalMs\": {\n          \"anyOf\": [\n            {\n              \"type\": \"number\",\n              \"const\": 0\n            },\n            {\n              \"type\": \"integer\",\n              \"minimum\": 60000,\n              \"maximum\": 9007199254740991\n            }\n          ]\n        }\n      },\n      \"required\": [\n        \"path\"\n      ]\n    },\n    \"fileSuggestion\": {\n      \"description\": \"Custom file suggestion configuration for @ mentions\",\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"type\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"const\": \"command\"\n        },\n        \"command\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\"\n        }\n      },\n      \"required\": [\n        \"type\",\n        \"command\"\n      ]\n    },\n    \"respectGitignore\": {\n      \"description\": \"Whether file picker should respect .gitignore files (default: true). Note: .ignore files are always respected.\",\n      \"type\": \"boolean\"\n    },\n    \"breakReminder\": {\n      \"description\": \"@internal Opt-in break reminder. When enabled, shows a dismissible nudge after sustained continuous use. Never blocks — just a friendly heads-up.\",\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"enabled\": {\n          \"description\": \"Show a friendly nudge after sustained continuous use (default false). Must be true for the reminder to fire.\",\n          \"type\": \"boolean\"\n        },\n        \"intervalMinutes\": {\n          \"description\": \"Minutes of continuous use before the reminder fires (default 120). Re-fires every interval until you take a break.\",\n          \"type\": \"integer\",\n          \"exclusiveMinimum\": 0,\n          \"maximum\": 9007199254740991\n        },\n        \"breakThresholdMinutes\": {\n          \"description\": \"Minutes of inactivity that count as a break and reset the timer (default 15)\",\n          \"type\": \"integer\",\n          \"exclusiveMinimum\": 0,\n          \"maximum\": 9007199254740991\n        },\n        \"message\": {\n          \"description\": \"Custom reminder text. Leave unset for a rotating set of friendly nudges.\",\n          \"type\": \"string\"\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    \"quietHours\": {\n      \"description\": \"@internal Opt-in quiet hours. When enabled, shows a single soft nudge per session while inside the configured local-time window. Never blocks.\",\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"enabled\": {\n          \"description\": \"Show a one-time nudge when you start or keep using the CLI inside your quiet-hours window (default false).\",\n          \"type\": \"boolean\"\n        },\n        \"start\": {\n          \"description\": \"Start of the quiet-hours window, 24-hour local time \\\"HH:MM\\\".\",\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"pattern\": \"^([01]?\\\\d|2[0-3]):[0-5]\\\\d$\"\n        },\n        \"end\": {\n          \"description\": \"End of the quiet-hours window, 24-hour local time \\\"HH:MM\\\". May be earlier than start for an overnight range.\",\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"pattern\": \"^([01]?\\\\d|2[0-3]):[0-5]\\\\d$\"\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    \"cleanupPeriodDays\": {\n      \"description\": \"Number of days to retain chat transcripts before automatic cleanup (default: 30). Minimum 1. Use a large value for long retention; use --no-session-persistence to disable transcript writes entirely.\",\n      \"type\": \"integer\",\n      \"exclusiveMinimum\": 0,\n      \"maximum\": 9007199254740991\n    },\n    \"skillListingMaxDescChars\": {\n      \"description\": \"Per-skill description character cap in the skill listing sent to Claude (default: 1536). Descriptions longer than this are truncated. Raise to opt in to higher per-turn context cost.\",\n      \"type\": \"integer\",\n      \"exclusiveMinimum\": 0,\n      \"maximum\": 9007199254740991\n    },\n    \"skillListingBudgetFraction\": {\n      \"description\": \"Fraction of the context window (in characters) reserved for the skill listing sent to Claude (default: 0.01 = 1%). When the listing exceeds this, descriptions are shortened to fit. Raise to opt in to higher per-turn context cost.\",\n      \"type\": \"number\",\n      \"exclusiveMinimum\": 0,\n      \"maximum\": 1\n    },\n    \"wslInheritsWindowsSettings\": {\n      \"description\": \"When set to true in either admin-only Windows source — the HKLM SOFTWARE/Policies/ClaudeCode registry key or C:/Program Files/ClaudeCode/managed-settings.json — WSL reads managed settings from the full Windows policy chain (HKLM, C:/Program Files/ClaudeCode via DrvFs, HKCU) in addition to /etc/claude-code. Windows sources take priority. The flag is also required in HKCU itself for HKCU policy to apply on WSL (double opt-in: admin enables the chain, user confirms HKCU). On native Windows the flag has no effect.\",\n      \"type\": \"boolean\"\n    },\n    \"env\": {\n      \"description\": \"Environment variables to set for Claude Code sessions\",\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"propertyNames\": {\n        \"type\": \"string\"\n      },\n      \"additionalProperties\": {\n        \"type\": \"string\"\n      }\n    },\n    \"attribution\": {\n      \"description\": \"Customize attribution text for commits and PRs. Each field defaults to the standard Claude Code attribution if not set.\",\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"commit\": {\n          \"description\": \"Attribution text for git commits, including any trailers. Empty string hides attribution.\",\n          \"type\": \"string\"\n        },\n        \"pr\": {\n          \"description\": \"Attribution text for pull request descriptions. Empty string hides attribution.\",\n          \"type\": \"string\"\n        }\n      }\n    },\n    \"includeCoAuthoredBy\": {\n      \"description\": \"Deprecated: Use attribution instead. \"items\": {\n          \"type\": \"object\",\n          \"properties\": {\n            \"matcher\": {\n              \"description\": \"String pattern to match (e.g. tool names like \"type\": \"array\",\n              \"items\": {\n                \"anyOf\": [\n                  {\n                    \"type\": \"object\",\n                    \"properties\": {\n                      \"type\": {\n                        \"description\": \"Shell command hook type\",\n                        \"type\": \"string\",\n                        \"const\": \"command\"\n                      },\n                      \"command\": {\n                        \"description\": \"Shell command to execute\",\n                        \"type\": \"string\"\n                      },\n                      \"args\": {\n                        \"description\": \"Argument list for exec form. When absent, `command` runs through a shell (bash on POSIX, PowerShell on Windows without Git Bash).\",\n                        \"type\": \"array\",\n                        \"items\": {\n                          \"type\": \"string\"\n                        }\n                      },\n                      \"if\": {\n                        \"description\": \"Permission rule syntax to filter when this hook runs (e.g., \\\"Bash(git *)\\\"). 'bash' uses your $SHELL (bash/zsh/sh); 'powershell' uses pwsh. Array form locks specific surfaces (e.g. [\\\"skills\\\", \\\"hooks\\\"]); `true` locks all four; `false` is an explicit no-op. Composes with strictKnownMarketplaces for end-to-end admin control — plugins gated by marketplace allowlist, everything else blocked here.\",\n      \"anyOf\": [\n        {\n          \"type\": \"boolean\"\n        },\n        {\n          \"type\": \"array\",\n          \"items\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\",\n            \"enum\": [\n              \"skills\",\n              \"agents\",\n              \"hooks\",\n              \"mcp\"\n            ]\n          }\n        }\n      ]\n    },\n    \"statusLine\": {\n      \"description\": \"Custom status line display configuration\",\n      \"type\": \"object\",\n      \"properties\": {\n        \"type\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\",\n          \"const\": \"command\"\n        },\n        \"command\": {\n          \"type\": \"string\"\n        },\n        \"padding\": {\n          \"type\": \"number\"\n        },\n        \"refreshInterval\": {\n          \"description\": \"Re-run the status line command every N seconds in addition to event-driven updates\",\n          \"type\": \"number\",\n          \"minimum\": 1\n        },\n        \"hideVimModeIndicator\": {\n          \"description\": \"Hide the built-in `-- INSERT --` / `-- VISUAL --` indicator below the prompt. Example: { \\\"formatter@anthropic-tools\\\": true }. Also supports extended format with version constraints. suggestions (relevance-based tips), in addition to the official marketplace. Only honored when set in managed settings (policy scope); the key is ignored in user, project, and local settings. A name only takes effect when the marketplace is registered on the machine AND its registered source is also declared in managed settings, either as the extraKnownMarketplaces entry for that name or as an entry of strictKnownMarketplaces. A marketplace registered from a different source under an allowlisted name is ignored.\",\n      \"type\": \"array\",\n      \"items\": {\n        \"type\": \"string\"\n      }\n    },\n    \"forceLoginMethod\": {\n      \"description\": \"Force a specific login method: \\\"claudeai\\\" for Claude Pro/Max, \\\"console\\\" for Console billing, \\\"gateway\\\" for the Cloud gateway OIDC device flow\",\n      \"type\": \"string\",\n      \"enum\": [\n        \"claudeai\",\n        \"console\",\n        \"gateway\"\n      ]\n    },\n    \"forceLoginGatewayUrl\": {\n      \"description\": \"@internal Cloud gateway URL to pre-fill and auto-connect to during login. Typically set in local managed settings alongside forceLoginMethod: \\\"gateway\\\" so users never type the URL. Has no effect when no admin tier exists (parent applies as the sole policy tier, still filtered restrictive-only).\",\n      \"type\": \"string\",\n      \"enum\": [\n        \"first-wins\",\n        \"merge\"\n      ]\n    },\n    \"forceLoginOrgUUID\": {\n      \"description\": \"Organization UUID to require for OAuth login. When false (default), a warning is shown and commands run unsandboxed. Intended for managed-settings deployments that require sandboxing as a hard gate.\",\n          \"type\": \"boolean\"\n        },\n        \"autoAllowBashIfSandboxed\": {\n          \"type\": \"boolean\"\n        },\n        \"allowUnsandboxedCommands\": {\n          \"description\": \"Allow commands to run outside the sandbox via the dangerouslyDisableSandbox parameter. Default: true.\",\n          \"type\": \"boolean\"\n        },\n        \"network\": {\n          \"type\": \"object\",\n          \"properties\": {\n            \"allowedDomains\": {\n              \"type\": \"array\",\n              \"items\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\"\n              }\n            },\n            \"deniedDomains\": {\n              \"description\": \"Domains that are always blocked, even if matched by allowedDomains. User, project, local, and flag settings domains are ignored. Denied domains are still respected from all sources.\",\n              \"type\": \"boolean\"\n            },\n            \"allowUnixSockets\": {\n              \"description\": \"macOS only: Unix socket paths to allow. Ignored on Linux (seccomp cannot filter by path).\",\n              \"type\": \"array\",\n              \"items\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\"\n              }\n            },\n            \"allowAllUnixSockets\": {\n              \"description\": \"If true, allow all Unix sockets (disables blocking on both platforms).\",\n              \"type\": \"boolean\"\n            },\n            \"allowLocalBinding\": {\n              \"type\": \"boolean\"\n            },\n            \"allowMachLookup\": {\n              \"description\": \"macOS only: Additional XPC/Mach service names to allow looking up. Supports trailing-wildcard prefix matching (e.g., \\\"com.apple.coresimulator.*\\\"). Needed for tools that communicate via XPC such as the iOS Simulator or Playwright.\",\n              \"type\": \"array\",\n              \"items\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\"\n              }\n            },\n            \"httpProxyPort\": {\n              \"type\": \"number\"\n            },\n            \"socksProxyPort\": {\n              \"type\": \"number\"\n            },\n            \"tlsTerminate\": {\n              \"description\": \"[EXPERIMENTAL] Enable in-process TLS termination so the per-request filter can see HTTPS request bodies. Provide a CA cert+key, or omit both to have sandbox-runtime generate an ephemeral one for the session.\",\n              \"type\": \"object\",\n              \"properties\": {\n                \"caCertPath\": {\n                  \"type\": \"string\",\n                  \"minLength\": 1\n                },\n                \"caKeyPath\": {\n                  \"type\": \"string\",\n                  \"minLength\": 1\n                }\n              }\n            }\n          }\n        },\n        \"filesystem\": {\n          \"type\": \"object\",\n          \"properties\": {\n            \"allowWrite\": {\n              \"description\": \"Additional paths to allow writing within the sandbox. Merged with paths from Edit(...) allow permission rules.\",\n              \"type\": \"array\",\n              \"items\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\"\n              }\n            },\n            \"denyWrite\": {\n              \"description\": \"Additional paths to deny writing within the sandbox. Merged with paths from Edit(...) deny permission rules.\",\n              \"type\": \"array\",\n              \"items\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\"\n              }\n            },\n            \"denyRead\": {\n              \"description\": \"Additional paths to deny reading within the sandbox. Merged with paths from Read(...) deny permission rules.\",\n              \"type\": \"array\",\n              \"items\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\"\n              }\n            },\n            \"allowRead\": {\n              \"description\": \"Paths to re-allow reading within denyRead regions. Takes precedence over denyRead for matching paths.\",\n              \"type\": \"array\",\n              \"items\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\"\n              }\n            },\n            \"allowManagedReadPathsOnly\": {\n              \"description\": \"When true (set in managed settings), only allowRead paths from policySettings are used.\",\n              \"type\": \"boolean\"\n            }\n          }\n        },\n        \"ignoreViolations\": {\n          \"type\": \"object\",\n          \"propertyNames\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\"\n          },\n          \"additionalProperties\": {\n            \"type\": \"array\",\n            \"items\": {\n              \"type\": \"string\"\n            }\n          }\n        },\n        \"enableWeakerNestedSandbox\": {\n          \"type\": \"boolean\"\n        },\n        \"enableWeakerNetworkIsolation\": {\n          \"description\": \"macOS only: Allow access to com.apple.trustd.agent in the sandbox. Default: false\",\n          \"type\": \"boolean\"\n        },\n        \"excludedCommands\": {\n          \"type\": \"array\",\n          \"items\": {\n            \"type\": \"string\"\n          }\n        },\n        \"ripgrep\": {\n          \"description\": \"Custom ripgrep configuration for bundled ripgrep support\",\n          \"type\": \"object\",\n          \"properties\": {\n            \"command\": {\n              \"type\": \"string\"\n            },\n            \"args\": {\n              \"type\": \"array\",\n              \"items\": {\n                \"type\": \"string\"\n              }\n            }\n          },\n          \"required\": [\n            \"command\"\n          ]\n        },\n        \"bwrapPath\": {\n          \"description\": \"Linux/WSL only: Absolute path to the bwrap (bubblewrap) binary.
