גוניור\\.claude\\CLAUDE.md","content":{"path":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\.claude\\CLAUDE.md","type":"Project","content":"# עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\n\n[One-line description of the project goal and target audience]\n\n---\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- Framework: [e.g., Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript]\n- Styling: [e.g., Tailwind CSS, CSS Modules]\n- Backend: [e.g., Node.js, PostgreSQL, Prisma]\n- Node version: [e.g., 20.x]\n- Package manager: [npm / pnpm / yarn]\n\n---\n\n## Key Commands\n\n```\nnpm run dev          — start dev server\nnpm run build        — production build\nnpm test             — run test suite\nnpm run lint         — lint\nnpm run typecheck    — TypeScript check (run after every change)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\nsrc/\n  components/    — reusable UI components\n  pages/         — route-level pages\n  lib/           — utilities and shared logic\n  styles/        — global styles and design tokens\npublic/          — static assets\n```\n\n---\n\n## Core Files\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|---|---|\n| `src/lib/api.ts` | Central API client |\n| `src/components/Layout.tsx` | Root layout and nav |\n| `.env.example` | All required env vars (copy to `.env.local`) |\n\n---\n\n## Non-Negotiables\n\n- Never commit secrets, API keys, or `.env` files.\n- Never skip typechecking — run `npm run typecheck` after every non-trivial change.\n- Never add a dependency without checking bundle impact first.\n- No `console.log` in committed code.\n- `main` is protected — work on feature branches, open a PR for review.\n\n---\n\n## Architecture Notes\n\n- [Describe high-level data flow]\n- [Note hard constraints]\n- [Call out seams that are easy to break]\n\n---\n\n## Known Quirks\n\n- [Surprising behaviors — high-value institutional memory]\n\n---\n\n@../rules/code-style.md\n@../rules/frontend.md\n@../rules/testing.md\n@../rules/git-workflow.md\n@../rules/performance.md\n@../rules/prompt-engineering.md\n","contentDiffersFromDisk":false},"displayPath":".claude\\CLAUDE.md"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"98a584f9-f1cb-4c55-a790-691a03d4b096","timestamp":"2026-06-06T19:01:06.334Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור","sessionId":"368947d7-a64b-4fc9-a2f6-fccfabe83369","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"master"}
{"parentUuid":"98a584f9-f1cb-4c55-a790-691a03d4b096","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"nested_memory","path":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\rules\\code-style.md","content":{"path":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\rules\\code-style.md","type":"Project","content":"# Code Style\n\n## TypeScript\n\n- Enable strict mode (`\"strict\": true` in tsconfig). No exceptions.\n- No `any` — use `unknown` and narrow, or define a proper type.\n- Prefer `type` over `interface` for shapes that won't be extended; use `interface` for public APIs that consumers may augment.\n- Never use non-null assertion (`!`) on values you haven't verified are non-null.\n- Prefer explicit return types on exported functions; infer for internal/trivial cases.\n\n## Naming\n\n- Files: `PascalCase` for components (`UserCard.tsx`), `camelCase` for utilities (`formatDate.ts`), `kebab-case` for route pages (`user-profile.tsx` in Next.js App Router).\n- Variables/functions: `camelCase`.\n- Constants and env config values: `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE`.\n- Boolean variables/props: prefix with `is`, `has`, `can`, `should` (`isLoading`, `hasError`).\n- Event handlers: prefix with `handle` (`handleSubmit`, `handleChange`).\n\n## Imports\n\nOrder (separated by blank lines, no mixing):\n1. Node built-ins (`node:path`, `node:fs`)\n2. External packages (`react`, `next`, third-party libs)\n3. Internal aliases (`@/lib/...`, `@/components/...`)\n4. Relative imports (`../utils`, `./types`)\n\nUse named exports everywhere. No default exports — they hurt refactoring and auto-import.\n\n## Functions\n\n- Small, single-purpose. If a function needs more than ~30 lines, look for an extraction.\n- Prefer pure functions. Side effects should be at the boundary (event handlers, effects, API calls).\n- No magic numbers — name them as constants.\n\n## Comments\n\n- Only for non-obvious *why*, never for *what*.\n- No commented-out code — delete it, git has history.\n- No TODO comments in committed code unless paired with a ticket reference.\n\n## Formatting\n\n- Prettier handles formatting — don't fight it.\n- Max line length: 100 characters.\n- Trailing commas in multi-line arrays/objects/params (Prettier default).\n","contentDiffersFromDisk":false,"parent":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\.claude\\CLAUDE.md"},"displayPath":"rules\\code-style.md"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"9c4d4080-739a-4a5c-a6c5-987f3f17f584","timestamp":"2026-06-06T19:01:06.334Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור","sessionId":"368947d7-a64b-4fc9-a2f6-fccfabe83369","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"master"}
{"parentUuid":"9c4d4080-739a-4a5c-a6c5-987f3f17f584","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"nested_memory","path":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\rules\\frontend.md","content":{"path":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\rules\\frontend.md","type":"Project","content":"# Frontend\n\n## React Components\n\n- One component per file. File name matches the component name.\n- Keep components under ~150 lines — extract subcomponents or custom hooks if larger.\n- Props interface defined in the same file, just above the component.\n- No prop drilling beyond 2 levels — use Context or a state manager.\n- Avoid `React.FC` — use plain function declarations with typed props.\n\n```tsx\n// preferred\nfunction UserCard({ name, email }: UserCardProps) { ... }\n\n// avoid\nconst UserCard: React.FC<UserCardProps> = ({ name, email }) => { ... }\n```\n\n## Hooks\n\n- Extract logic into custom hooks when a component has more than one `useEffect` or complex derived state.\n- Custom hook files: `use` prefix, `camelCase` (`useAuthSession.ts`).\n- Never call hooks conditionally or inside loops.\n- `useEffect` must have a complete dependency array. If lint warns, fix the dependency — don't suppress it.\n- Avoid `useEffect` for derived state — compute it inline or with `useMemo`.\n\n## State\n\n- Keep state as close to where it's used as possible — lift only when necessary.\n- Prefer `useReducer` over multiple related `useState` calls when state transitions are complex.\n- Server state (fetched data) belongs in a data-fetching library (React Query, SWR, Next.js cache) — not in `useState`.\n\n## Accessibility\n\n- Always use semantic HTML: `<button>` for actions, `<a>` for navigation, `<nav>`, `<main>`, `<section>` etc.\n- Interactive elements must be keyboard accessible (focus ring visible, `Enter`/`Space` fire the action).\n- Images must have descriptive `alt` text. Decorative images: `alt=\"\"`.\n- Form inputs must have associated `<label>` (via `htmlFor` or wrapping).\n- Use `aria-label` only when visible text isn't available — prefer visible labels.\n- Color contrast must meet WCAG AA (4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for UI components).\n\n## CSS / Styling\n\n- [Tailwind / CSS Modules / styled-components — fill in your choice and remove the others]\n- No inline styles except for truly dynamic values (e.g., `style={{ width: progress + '%' }}`).\n- Design tokens (colors, spacing, radii) live in one place — don't hardcode hex values.\n- Mobile-first breakpoints. Start with the small viewport, add larger overrides.\n- Avoid `z-index` values above 50 without documenting why in a comment.\n\n## Images & Media\n\n- Always use `next/image` (or framework equivalent) for optimized loading.\n- Provide `width` and `height` to prevent layout shift.\n- Use `loading=\"lazy\"` for images below the fold.\n- Prefer SVG for icons; use an icon component system rather than ad-hoc inline SVGs.\n\n## Error & Loading States\n\nEvery data-dependent UI must handle three states explicitly:\n1. **Loading** — skeleton, spinner, or placeholder\n2. **Error** — user-friendly message + retry action where applicable\n3. **Empty** — intentional empty state, not just \"nothing rendered\"\n\nNever leave a component that silently renders nothing on error.\n","contentDiffersFromDisk":false,"parent":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\.claude\\CLAUDE.md"},"displayPath":"rules\\frontend.md"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"190aa141-5871-48ec-8f95-1ac7fafabea9","timestamp":"2026-06-06T19:01:06.335Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור","sessionId":"368947d7-a64b-4fc9-a2f6-fccfabe83369","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"master"}
{"parentUuid":"190aa141-5871-48ec-8f95-1ac7fafabea9","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"nested_memory","path":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\rules\\testing.md","content":{"path":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\rules\\testing.md","type":"Project","content":"# Testing\n\n## Framework\n\nUse [Vitest / Jest — pick one and fill in]. Never introduce a second test runner.\nComponent tests: [React Testing Library]. E2E: [Playwright / Cypress — pick one].\n\n## What to Test\n\n- **Unit test**: pure utility functions, complex business logic, edge-case branches.\n- **Component test**: user-visible behavior — what the user sees and does, not implementation details.\n- **E2E test**: critical user journeys (sign-up, checkout, primary feature flow).\n\nDo not test:\n- Framework internals (React re-renders, routing internals).\n- Implementation details (private functions, internal state shape).\n- Code that is already covered by third-party library tests.\n\n## Structure (AAA)\n\n```ts\nit('shows error message when login fails', async () => {\n  // Arrange\n  server.use(http.post('/api/login', () => HttpResponse.json({ error: 'Invalid credentials' }, { status: 401 })))\n\n  // Act\n  render(<LoginForm />)\n  await userEvent.type(screen.getByLabelText('Email'), 'user@example.com')\n  await userEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Sign in' }))\n\n  // Assert\n  expect(screen.getByRole('alert')).toHaveTextContent('Invalid credentials')\n})\n```\n\n## Conventions\n\n- Test files: co-located with source (`UserCard.test.tsx` next to `UserCard.tsx`), or in `__tests__/`.\n- Test names: describe user behavior, not function names. `'shows error when...'` not `'handleSubmit throws'`.\n- One logical assertion per test. Multiple `expect` calls are fine when they verify the same behavior.\n- Use `userEvent` over `fireEvent` — it simulates real browser interactions.\n- Don't test styles (class names). Test visibility, text content, and ARIA attributes.\n- Mock at the network boundary (MSW), not at the module level, unless unavoidable.\n\n## Coverage\n\n- 100% coverage is not the goal — confidence is.\n- Every bug fix should come with a regression test.\n- Run the full suite before opening a PR.\n","contentDiffersFromDisk":false,"parent":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\.claude\\CLAUDE.md"},"displayPath":"rules\\testing.md"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"a5e1f9a7-e1fe-42ea-bb23-e2fc853fa48a","timestamp":"2026-06-06T19:01:06.335Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור","sessionId":"368947d7-a64b-4fc9-a2f6-fccfabe83369","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"master"}
{"parentUuid":"a5e1f9a7-e1fe-42ea-bb23-e2fc853fa48a","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"nested_memory","path":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\rules\\git-workflow.md","content":{"path":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\rules\\git-workflow.md","type":"Project","content":"# Git Workflow\n\n## Branches\n\n- `main` is protected. Never push directly.\n- Branch naming: `<type>/<short-description>` — e.g., `feat/user-avatar`, `fix/login-redirect`, `chore/update-deps`.\n- Types: `feat`, `fix`, `chore`, `refactor`, `docs`, `test`.\n- Delete branches after merge.\n\n## Commits\n\nFormat: `<type>: <short imperative summary>` (max 72 chars)\n\n```\nfeat: add avatar upload to profile page\nfix: prevent double-submit on checkout form\nchore: upgrade eslint to v9\n```\n\n- One logical change per commit.\n- Present tense, imperative mood: \"add\" not \"added\", \"fix\" not \"fixes\".\n- Reference issue/ticket in the body when relevant: `Closes #42`.\n- Never commit: secrets, `.env`, `node_modules`, build artifacts, `console.log`.\n\n## Pull Requests\n\n- One concern per PR. If you're fixing a bug and you spot a refactor opportunity, open a separate PR.\n- PR title follows the same format as commit messages.\n- Fill in the PR description: what changed, why, and how to test it.\n- All CI checks must pass before requesting review.\n- Squash merge into `main` — keep the history clean.\n- At least one approval required before merging.\n\n## Code Review\n\n- Review the diff, not just the final state.\n- Suggest, don't dictate — use \"consider\" and \"what do you think about\" for non-blocking feedback.\n- Blocking issues must be resolved before merge; non-blocking notes can be addressed in a follow-up.\n- Don't approve PRs you haven't actually read.\n","contentDiffersFromDisk":false,"parent":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\.claude\\CLAUDE.md"},"displayPath":"rules\\git-workflow.md"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"eee2fbeb-cb4f-409a-916c-160d2b88690d","timestamp":"2026-06-06T19:01:06.336Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור","sessionId":"368947d7-a64b-4fc9-a2f6-fccfabe83369","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"master"}
{"parentUuid":"eee2fbeb-cb4f-409a-916c-160d2b88690d","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"nested_memory","path":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\rules\\performance.md","content":{"path":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\rules\\performance.md","type":"Project","content":"# Performance\n\n## Bundle Size\n\n- Check bundle impact before adding any dependency: `npm run build` and inspect the output, or use `bundlephobia.com`.\n- Prefer smaller focused packages over large ones (e.g., `date-fns` over `moment`, `zod` over `yup`).\n- Never import an entire library when you need one function: `import { debounce } from 'lodash-es'` not `import _ from 'lodash'`.\n- Tree-shaking only works with ES modules — avoid CJS-only packages where possible.\n\n## Code Splitting\n\n- Lazy-load route-level components: `const Page = lazy(() => import('./Page'))`.\n- Heavy components (rich text editors, charts, map libraries) must be lazy-loaded.\n- Never lazy-load components that appear above the fold on first load — it causes visible pop-in.\n\n## Rendering\n\n- Avoid unnecessary re-renders:\n  - Don't create new objects/arrays/functions inline in JSX props if the child is memoized.\n  - `useMemo` and `useCallback` are not free — only use them when you have a measured problem or a stable reference is required (e.g., passing to a memoized child or `useEffect` dependency).\n- Avoid reading from the DOM in a render cycle (`getBoundingClientRect`, `scrollTop`) — use `useLayoutEffect` if unavoidable.\n- Lists with 50+ items should be virtualized (`react-virtual`, `tanstack-virtual`).\n\n## Images\n\n- Use next-gen formats (WebP, AVIF).\n- Always provide `width` and `height` to prevent Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS).\n- Hero images: `fetchpriority=\"high\"`, `loading=\"eager\"`. All others: `loading=\"lazy\"`.\n\n## Web Vitals Targets\n\n| Metric | Target |\n|---|---|\n| LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) | < 2.5s |\n| CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) | < 0.1 |\n| INP (Interaction to Next Paint) | < 200ms |\n\nMeasure with Lighthouse CI or the browser DevTools Performance panel before shipping features that change the critical rendering path.\n","contentDiffersFromDisk":false,"parent":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\.claude\\CLAUDE.md"},"displayPath":"rules\\performance.md"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"7c7c78f2-f839-45b2-a1b3-151a5e11628c","timestamp":"2026-06-06T19:01:06.336Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור","sessionId":"368947d7-a64b-4fc9-a2f6-fccfabe83369","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"master"}
{"parentUuid":"7c7c78f2-f839-45b2-a1b3-151a5e11628c","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"nested_memory","path":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\rules\\prompt-engineering.md","content":{"path":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\rules\\prompt-engineering.md","type":"Project","content":"# Prompt Engineering\n\n## Core Principles\n\n- Be explicit about the task, context, and desired output format upfront.\n- One prompt, one goal — don't bundle multiple unrelated tasks into a single prompt.\n- Show, don't just tell: include examples (few-shot) when the format or behavior is non-obvious.\n- Specify constraints explicitly: length, tone, language, what to avoid.\n\n## Prompt Structure\n\nPreferred order for complex prompts:\n\n1. **Role / context** — who the model is or what it knows\n2. **Task** — what it must do, in one sentence\n3. **Input** — the data or text to work with\n4. **Constraints** — format, length, things to avoid\n5. **Output format** — exact structure expected (JSON, markdown, list, etc.)\n\n```\nYou are a senior copywriter for a glass-design company.\nTask: Write a 3-sentence product description for the item below.\nInput: [product details]\nConstraints: No superlatives, use second-person (\"you\"), max 60 words.\nOutput: Plain paragraph, no headers or bullets.\n```\n\n## System vs User Messages\n\n- System message: persistent identity, behavioral rules, output format contracts.\n- User message: the actual task and input data.\n- Never mix identity instructions into user messages — they get overridden easily.\n\n## Few-Shot Examples\n\n- Use 2–3 examples when the output pattern is non-trivial.\n- Examples must match the exact format you want back — inconsistent examples produce inconsistent output.\n- Order matters: put the most representative example last (recency bias).\n\n## Chaining\n\n- Break multi-step reasoning into sequential prompts — don't ask the model to do 5 things in one shot.\n- Pass only the relevant output of step N into step N+1; don't carry the full history.\n- For classification → extraction → rewrite pipelines: validate the output of each step before moving on.\n\n## Temperature & Sampling\n\n| Use case | Temperature |\n|---|---|\n| Data extraction, classification | 0 – 0.2 |\n| Copywriting, product descriptions | 0.5 – 0.8 |\n| Brainstorming, ideation | 0.9 – 1.2 |\n\n- Keep temperature at 0 for anything that must be deterministic (structured data, code).\n- Don't raise temperature to \"fix\" bad prompts — fix the prompt first.\n\n## Variables in Prompts\n\n- Mark dynamic inputs clearly: `{{product_name}}`, `{{user_input}}`.\n- Sanitize user-supplied content before interpolating — prevent prompt injection.\n- Never concatenate raw user input directly into a system message.\n\n## Evaluation\n\n- Write an eval set (10–20 examples) before iterating on a production prompt.\n- A prompt change that improves one case may regress another — always test the full set.\n- Log prompt + input + output in production for offline analysis.\n\n## Common Mistakes\n\n- **Under-specifying format**: \"Give me a list\" is ambiguous — say \"numbered list, one item per line\".\n- **Over-prompting**: Adding 10 rules bloats the prompt and the model starts ignoring some.\n- **Vague negatives**: \"Don't be too formal\" — say \"Use casual tone, as if texting a colleague\".\n- **No grounding**: Asking for facts without providing source material leads to hallucinations.\n- **Changing two things at once**: When iterating, change one variable per test so you know what worked.\n","contentDiffersFromDisk":false,"parent":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\\.claude\\CLAUDE.md"},"displayPath":"rules\\prompt-engineering.md"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"cee6cb84-f1cc-447c-869f-bb13cdcf646e","timestamp":"2026-06-06T19:01:06.336Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור","sessionId":"368947d7-a64b-4fc9-a2f6-fccfabe83369","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"master"}

Name it \"ריוי - בדיקת קוד יומית\""},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2949,"cache_read_input_tokens":128044,"output_tokens":141,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2949,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"not_available","iterations":[{"input_tokens":1,"output_tokens":141,"cache_read_input_tokens":128044,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2949,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2949},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"},"diagnostics":null},"requestId":"req_011CbnXhWhTLSNPqcosVPpnn","attributionSkill":"update-config","type":"assistant","uuid":"e31bdfde-8a2b-4dcf-85fc-fda21bc1672e","timestamp":"2026-06-06T19:07:40.818Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור","sessionId":"368947d7-a64b-4fc9-a2f6-fccfabe83369","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"master"}

{"parentUuid":"e7830898-3e93-4d09-8e9d-afaa1364020e","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"9e618a17-1618-45de-8339-c6425b3260b2","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"# Schedule Remote Agents\n\nYou are helping the user schedule, update, list, or run **remote** Claude Code agents. These are NOT local cron jobs — each routine spawns a fully isolated remote session (CCR) in Anthropic's cloud infrastructure, either on a recurring cron schedule or once at a specific time. The agent runs in a sandboxed environment with its own git checkout, tools, and optional MCP connections.\n\n## First Step\n\nThe user has already told you what they want (see User Request at the bottom). Skip the initial question and go directly to the matching workflow.\n\n## Setup Notes\n\n⚠ Heads-up:\n- Not in a git repo — you'll need to specify a repo URL manually (or skip repos entirely).\n\n\n## What You Can Do\n\nUse the `RemoteTrigger` tool (load it first with `ToolSearch select:RemoteTrigger`; auth is handled in-process — do not use curl):\n\n- `{action: \"list\"}` — list all routines\n- `{action: \"get\", trigger_id: \"...\"}` — fetch one routine\n- `{action: \"create\", body: {...}}` — create a routine\n- `{action: \"update\", trigger_id: \"...\", body: {...}}` — partial update\n- `{action: \"run\", trigger_id: \"...\"}` — run a routine now\n\n(Note: the API uses `trigger_id` as the parameter name, but the user-facing term is \"routine\".)\n\nYou CANNOT delete routines. If the user asks to delete, direct them to: https://claude.ai/code/routines\n\n## Create body shape\n\nFor a recurring schedule:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"name\": \"AGENT_NAME\",\n  \"cron_expression\": \"CRON_EXPR\",\n  \"enabled\": true,\n  \"job_config\": {\n    \"ccr\": {\n      \"environment_id\": \"ENVIRONMENT_ID\",\n      \"session_context\": {\n        \"model\": \"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\n        \"sources\": [\n          {\"git_repository\": {\"url\": \"https://github.com/ORG/REPO\"}}\n        ],\n        \"allowed_tools\": [\"Bash\", \"Read\", \"Write\", \"Edit\", \"Glob\", \"Grep\"]\n      },\n      \"events\": [\n        {\"data\": {\n          \"uuid\": \"<lowercase v4 uuid>\",\n          \"session_id\": \"\",\n          \"type\": \"user\",\n          \"parent_tool_use_id\": null,\n          \"message\": {\"content\": \"PROMPT_HERE\", \"role\": \"user\"}\n        }}\n      ]\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\nFor a one-time run, replace `\"cron_expression\": \"CRON_EXPR\"` with `\"run_once_at\": \"YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ\"` (RFC3339 UTC, must be in the future). Everything else is identical.\n\nGenerate a fresh lowercase UUID for `events[].data.uuid` yourself.\n\n## Available MCP Connectors\n\nThese are the user's currently connected claude.ai MCP connectors:\n\nConnected connectors (available for routines):\n- claude.ai Microsoft Learn (connector_uuid: 3e7087fe-69d7-4b10-91bd-d4c343865574, name: Microsoft-Learn, url: https://learn.microsoft.com/api/mcp)\n- claude.ai Gmail (connector_uuid: 237a989c-40b8-4e32-9e3f-5b9dd4494080, name: Gmail, url: https://gmailmcp.googleapis.com/mcp/v1)\n\nWhen attaching connectors to a routine, use the `connector_uuid` and `name` shown above (the name is already sanitized to only contain letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores), and the connector's URL. The `name` field in `mcp_connections` must only contain `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]` — dots and spaces are NOT allowed.\n\n**Important:** Infer what services the agent needs from the user's description. For example, if they say \"check Datadog and Slack me errors,\" the agent needs both Datadog and Slack connectors. Cross-reference against the list above and warn if any required service isn't connected. If a needed connector is missing, direct the user to https://claude.ai/customize/connectors to connect it first.\n\n## Environments\n\nEvery routine requires an `environment_id` in the job config. This determines where the remote agent runs. Ask the user which environment to use.\n\nAvailable environments:\n- Default (id: env_01DvdD4CmfyVhdq3XpKx8PA2, kind: anthropic_cloud)\n\nUse the `id` value as the `environment_id` in `job_config.ccr.environment_id`.\n\n**Note:** A new environment `Default` (id: `env_01DvdD4CmfyVhdq3XpKx8PA2`) was just created for the user because they had none. Use this id for `job_config.ccr.environment_id` and mention the creation when you confirm the routine config.\n\n\n## API Field Reference\n\n### Create Routine — Required Fields\n- `name` (string) — A descriptive name\n- Exactly ONE of:\n  - `cron_expression` (string) — 5-field cron in UTC. **Minimum interval is 1 hour.**\n  - `run_once_at` (string) — RFC3339 UTC timestamp. Must be in the future. Fires once, then auto-disables.\n- `job_config` (object) — Session configuration (see structure above)\n\n### Create Routine — Optional Fields\n- `enabled` (boolean, default: true)\n- `mcp_connections` (array) — MCP servers to attach:\n  ```json\n  [{\"connector_uuid\": \"uuid\", \"name\": \"server-name\", \"url\": \"https://...\"}]\n  ```\n\n### Update Routine — Optional Fields\nAll fields optional (partial update):\n- `name`, `cron_expression`, `run_once_at`, `enabled`, `job_config`\n- `mcp_connections` — Replace MCP connections\n- `clear_mcp_connections` (boolean) — Remove all MCP connections\n\n### Cron Expression Examples\n\nThe user's local timezone is **Asia/Jerusalem**. Cron expressions and `run_once_at` timestamps are always in UTC. When the user says a local time, convert it to UTC but confirm with them: \"9am Asia/Jerusalem = Xam UTC, so the cron would be `0 X * * 1-5`.\" For one-time runs, the same conversion applies — \"run this at 3pm\" → `\"run_once_at\": \"YYYY-MM-DDTHH:00:00Z\"` with their 3pm converted to UTC.\n\n- `0 9 * * 1-5` — Every weekday at 9am **UTC**\n- `0 */2 * * *` — Every 2 hours\n- `0 0 * * *` — Daily at midnight **UTC**\n- `30 14 * * 1` — Every Monday at 2:30pm **UTC**\n- `0 8 1 * *` — First of every month at 8am **UTC**\n\nMinimum interval is 1 hour. `*/30 * * * *` will be rejected.\n\n### Current Time (for one-off runs)\n\nWhen /schedule was invoked it was **Sat, Jun 6, 2026, 10:10 PM** (Asia/Jerusalem) / **2026-06-06T19:10:14.684Z** UTC. Treat this as an approximate anchor only — the conversation may have been running for a while since then.\n\n**Before computing any `run_once_at` value, you MUST re-check the current time** by running `date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ` via the Bash tool. Do not guess or infer today's date from conversation context. Resolve relative requests (\"tomorrow at 9am\", \"in 3 hours\", \"next Monday\") against the freshly fetched time, then echo the resolved local time AND the UTC timestamp back to the user for confirmation before creating the routine. If the resolved time is already in the past, ask the user to clarify rather than silently rolling forward.\n\n## Workflow\n\n### CREATE a new routine:\n\n1. **Understand the goal** — Ask what they want the remote agent to do. What repo(s)? What task? Remind them that the agent runs remotely — it won't have access to their local machine, local files, or local environment variables.\n2. **Craft the prompt** — Help them write an effective agent prompt. Good prompts are:\n   - Specific about what to do and what success looks like\n   - Clear about which files/areas to focus on\n   - Explicit about what actions to take (open PRs, commit, just analyze, etc.)\n3. **Set the schedule** — Ask when and how often. The user's timezone is Asia/Jerusalem. When they say a time (e.g., \"every morning at 9am\"), assume they mean their local time and convert to UTC for the cron expression. Always confirm the conversion: \"9am Asia/Jerusalem = Xam UTC.\" If they want a one-time run (e.g., \"once at 3pm\", \"tomorrow morning\", \"remind me to check X later\"), use `run_once_at` instead of `cron_expression` — same timezone conversion applies. **First re-check the current time with `date -u` via Bash** (the reference time above may be stale in a long conversation), resolve the relative phrase against that fresh value, and confirm the resulting absolute timestamp with the user.\n4. **Choose the model** — Default to `claude-sonnet-4-6`. Tell the user which model you're defaulting to and ask if they want a different one.\n5. **Validate connections** — Infer what services the agent will need from the user's description. For example, if they say \"check Datadog and Slack me errors,\" the agent needs both Datadog and Slack MCP connectors. Cross-reference with the connectors list above. If any are missing, warn the user and link them to https://claude.ai/customize/connectors to connect first. Ask which git repos the remote agent needs cloned into its environment.\n6. **Review and confirm** — Show the full configuration before creating. Let them adjust.\n7. **Create it** — Call `RemoteTrigger` with `action: \"create\"` and show the result. The response includes the routine ID. Always output a link at the end: `https://claude.ai/code/routines/{ROUTINE_ID}`\n\n### UPDATE a routine:\n\n1. List routines first so they can pick one\n2. Ask what they want to change\n3. Show current vs proposed value\n4. Confirm and update\n\n### LIST routines:\n\n1. Fetch and display in a readable format\n2. Show: name, schedule (human-readable), enabled/disabled, next run, repo(s)\n\n### RUN NOW:\n\n1. List routines if they haven't specified which one\n2. Confirm which routine\n3. Execute and confirm\n\n## Important Notes\n\n- These are REMOTE agents — they run in Anthropic's cloud, not on the user's machine. They cannot access local files, local services, or local environment variables.\n- Always convert cron to human-readable when displaying\n- When listing routines, `ended_reason: \"run_once_fired\"` means a one-shot already ran (shows as \"Ran\" in the web UI). The user can re-arm it by updating with a new `run_once_at`.\n- Default to `enabled: true` unless user says otherwise\n- Accept GitHub URLs in any format (https://github.com/org/repo, org/repo, etc.) and normalize to the full HTTPS URL (without .git suffix)\n- The prompt is the most important part — spend time getting it right. The remote agent starts with zero context, so the prompt must be self-contained.\n- To delete a routine, direct users to https://claude.ai/code/routines\n\n\n## User Request\n\nThe user said: \"Create a daily routine that runs /code-review on the project at 9:00 AM every day. Name it \"ריוי - בדיקת קוד יומית\"\"\n\nStart by understanding their intent and working through the appropriate workflow above."}]},"isMeta":true,"uuid":"bf401547-e892-4a3e-90bb-0a3f21c60780","timestamp":"2026-06-06T19:10:14.700Z","sourceToolUseID":"toolu_01H3m45XHaswh9mTX2fBSBkA","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\עיצוב חזית הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור","sessionId":"368947d7-a64b-4fc9-a2f6-fccfabe83369","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"master"}
